title: allium sativum subsp. subrotundum
intro: Allium sativum subsp. subrotundum, also known as the 'Silverskin' or 'Silver White' garlic, is a subspecies of garlic that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. It is prized for its mild, sweet flavor and is often used in cooking to add depth and aroma to various dishes. The medicinal properties of Allium sativum subsp. subrotundum include its antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory infections, colds, and flu. This garlic subspecies can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which provide health benefits including reducing inflammation, lowering cholesterol levels, and supporting cardiovascular health.
